modifiers.name: Adrenaline
modifiers.description: Unlocks the Adrenaline skill (costs 1 [Action Point] and 20 [Fatigue] ) which puts you first in the turn order for the next round, to have another turn before your enemies do. Note: if two characters are using Adrenaline skill , the one with the highest [Initiative] Initiative will act first. The character attracts slightly more attention from enemies.

modifiers.name: Anticipation
modifiers.description: When being attacked with ranged weapons, gain 1 + 10% of your base Ranged Defense as additional [Ranged Defense] per tile that the attacker is away, bonus is minimum +10 [Ranged Defense] .

modifiers.name: Axe Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Split Shield damage to shields is increased by 50% when used with axes. Round Swing gains +5% chance to hit. The Longaxe no longer has a penalty for attacking targets directly adjacent.

modifiers.name: Backstabber
modifiers.description: The bonus to hitchance in melee is doubled to +10% for each ally (including your Hounds ) surrounding and distracting your target. Note: surrounding starts when two allies are adjacent to an enemy. See this section in Combat Mechanics and Hit Chance .

modifiers.name: Bags and Belts
modifiers.description: Unlock two extra bag slots to carry all your favorite things. Items placed in bags no longer give a penalty to Maximum Fatigue, except for two-handed weapons.

modifiers.name: Battle Forged
modifiers.description: Specialize in heavy armor: the heavier your armor and helmet, the more you benefit! Armor damage taken is reduced by a percentage equal to 5% of the current total armor value of both body and head armor. Does not affect damage from mental attacks or status effects.

modifiers.name: Berserk
modifiers.description: Once per turn, upon killing an enemy, 4 Action Points are immediately regained. Characters can not regain more than their maximum Action Points and no more than 4 for a single attack. The character attracts slightly more attention from opponents.

modifiers.name: Bow Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. [Vision] is increased by 1 . Maximum firing range with bows is increased by 1 .

modifiers.name: Brawny
modifiers.description: The [Fatigue] Fatigue and [Initiative] Initiative penalty from wearing armor and helmet is reduced by 30% .

modifiers.name: Bullseye
modifiers.description: The penalty to hit chance when shooting at a target that has no clear line of fire is reduced from 75% to 50% for ranged weapons. Note: this affects an initial, separate, hidden roll which determines whether the shot hits the intended target, and only applies when the intended target has cover. See Combat Mechanics and Hit Chance for further details.

modifiers.name: Cleaver Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Bleeding damage inflicted by cleavers and whips is doubled to 10 and 20 per turn, respectively. (bleeding damage is increased from 5 to 10 per stack) Disarm penalty to hit is reduced to -10% . (down from -20% )

modifiers.name: Colossus
modifiers.description: Hitpoints are increased by 25% , which also reduces the chance to sustain debilitating injuries when being hit. Note: the bonus is always retroactively updated with maximum [Hitpoints] .

modifiers.name: Crippling Strikes
modifiers.description: Lowers the threshold to inflict injuries by 33% for both melee and ranged attacks. Note: some enemies are immune to injuries or have very large [Health] health pool that protects them from receiving injuries.

modifiers.name: Crossbow Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. An additional 20% of damage inflicted with crossbows ignores armor. Handgonnes now require 6 [Action Points] to reload and can be fired every turn instead of every other turn.

modifiers.name: Dagger Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Stab, Puncture and Deathblow have a reduced Action Point cost (3 AP from 4) to allow for an additional attack each turn.

modifiers.name: Dodge
modifiers.description: Gain 15% of the character's current Initiative as a bonus to Melee and Ranged Defense.

modifiers.name: Duelist
modifiers.description: With the offhand free or carrying a throwable tool (e.g. a throwing net), an additional +25% of any damage ignores armor. Does not work with two-handed weapons.

modifiers.name: Executioner
modifiers.description: Inflict additional 20% [Damage] against targets that have sustained any injury effects, like a broken arm.

modifiers.name: Fast Adaptation
modifiers.description: Gain an additional stacking +10% chance to hit with each attack that misses an opponent. Bonus is reset upon landing a hit. Note: For the purposes of a reset on ranged attacks, hitting terrain blocking the target or a scattered shot counts as a hit and will reset the counter.

modifiers.name: Fearsome
modifiers.description: Make them scatter and flee! Any attack that inflicts at least 1 point of damage to hitpoints triggers a morale check for the opponent with a penalty equal to 15% of your Resolve, as opposed to with no penalty and only if at or above 15 points of damage. Note: only triggers once per target for a same skill use (however, for Orcs it is applied on each Charge vs Spearwall) [ 1 ] [ 2 ] . Bonus from Resolve is capped at 20.

modifiers.name: Flail Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Lash and Hail ignore the defense bonus of shield. Pound gains +10% [Direct Damage] on [a hit to the head] . Thresh gains +5% chance to hit.

modifiers.name: Footwork
modifiers.description: Unlocks the Footwork skill (costs 3 [Action Points] and 20 [Fatigue] ) which allows to move while being in the enemy Zone of Control or to leave it without triggering opportunity attacks.

modifiers.name: Fortified Mind
modifiers.description: Resolve is increased by 25% . Note: the bonus is always retroactively updated with maximum [Resolve] .

modifiers.name: Gifted
modifiers.description: Instantly gain a levelup to increase this character's attributes with maximum rolls but without talents.

modifiers.name: Hammer Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Destroy Armor and Demolish Armor inflict 33% more damage against armor. Shatter gains +5% chance to hit. The Polehammer no longer has a penalty for attacking targets directly adjacent.

modifiers.name: Head Hunter
modifiers.description: Hitting the head of a target will give you a guaranteed hit to the head also with your next attack. Connecting with your hit will reset the effect, but missing a hit - won't.

modifiers.name: Indomitable
modifiers.description: Unlocks the Indomitable skill (costs 5 [Action Points] and 25 [Fatigue] ) which grants a 50% [damage reduction] and immunity to being [Stunned] stunned, knocked back or grabbed for one turn. The character attracts less attention from opponents.

modifiers.name: Killing Frenzy
modifiers.description: A kill increases all damage by 25% for 2 turns. Does not stack, but another kill will reset the timer. The character attracts more attention from opponents. Note: the timer starts on the turn it activates, meaning that the buff will expire on the end of the character's next turn.

modifiers.name: Lone Wolf
modifiers.description: I work best alone. With no ally within 3 tiles of distance, gain a 15% bonus to Melee Skill, Ranged Skill, Melee Defense, Ranged Defense, and Resolve.

modifiers.name: Mace Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Knock Out , Knock Over and Strike Down have a 100% (+25%) chance to stun the target if not immune. The Polemace no longer has a penalty for attacking targets directly adjacent.

modifiers.name: Nimble
modifiers.description: Specialize in light armor: the lighter your armor and helmet, the more you benefit! Hitpoint damage taken is reduced by up to 60% , but lowered exponentially by the total penalty to Maximum Fatigue from body and head armor above 15 . Brawny does not affect this perk. Does not affect damage from mental attacks or status effects.

modifiers.name: Nine Lives
modifiers.description: Once per battle, upon receiving a killing blow, survive with a few [Health] Hitpoints left and receive +15 [Melee Defense] , +15 [Ranged Defense] , +15 [Resolve] , +15 [Initiative] until your next turn. All damage over time status effects (bleed, poison, etc) are removed. Note: [Health] Hitpoints gained are a random number between 11-15.

modifiers.name: Overwhelm
modifiers.description: Learn to take advantage of your high Initiative and prevent the enemy from attacking effectively by overwhelming them with your attacks! With every attack, hit or miss, against an opponent that acts after you in the current round, inflict the 'Overwhelmed' status effect which lowers both Melee Skill and Ranged Skill by 10% for one turn. The effect stacks with each attack, and can be applied to multiple targets at once with a single attack.

modifiers.name: Pathfinder
modifiers.description: Learn to move on difficult terrain. Action Point costs for movement on all terrain is reduced by -1 to a minimum of 2 [Action points] Action Points per tile, and [Fatigue] Fatigue cost is reduced to half. Changing height levels has no additional [Action points] Action Point cost anymore, but [Fatigue] Fatigue cost is unaffected.

modifiers.name: Polearm Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Polearm skills have their action point cost reduced to 5 , and no longer have a penalty for attacking targets directly adjacent.

modifiers.name: Quick Hands
modifiers.description: Swapping any item in battle except for shields becomes a free action with no Action Point cost once every turn. Note: This perk doesn't extend its effect for any actions with shields.

modifiers.name: Rally the Troops
modifiers.description: Unlocks the 'Rally' skill (costs 5 [Action Points] and 25 [Fatigue] ) which can rally fleeing allies, and raise morale of all nearby allies to a steady level. The higher the [Resolve] of the character using the skill, the higher the chance to succeed. Note: Rally triggers a morale check to raise morale of any ally at Wavering or worse morale within 4 tiles; with a bonus of 40% of the skill user's Resolve, and a penalty of -10 per tile of distance, to the roll. It can also rally fleeing allies within the same range at no penalty. Character attracts moderately more attention from enemies.

modifiers.name: Reach Advantage
modifiers.description: Each hit with a two-handed melee weapon adds a stack of Reach Advantage that increases your Melee Defense by 5 , up to a maximum of 5 stacks, until this character's next turn. A single attack hitting multiple targets can add several stacks at once. If you put away your weapon, you lose all stacks.

modifiers.name: Recover
modifiers.description: Unlocks the [Recover] Recover skill which allows to reduce accumulated [Fatigue] Fatigue by 50% by resting for the current turn (costs 9 [Action Points] ).

modifiers.name: Relentless
modifiers.description: At all times your [Initiative] Initiative is reduced only by 50% of your accumulated [Fatigue] Fatigue, instead of all of it. Using the 'Wait' command no longer gives you a −25% penalty to [Initiative] Initiative when determining the turn order on the next round.

modifiers.name: Resilient
modifiers.description: Any negative status effect with a finite duration (e.g. Bleeding , Charmed ) has its duration reduced to 1 turn. Status effects that have their effects grow weaker over several turns (e.g. Goblin Poison ) are at their weakest state from the start.

modifiers.name: Rotation
modifiers.description: Unlocks the Rotation skill (costs 3 [Action Points] and 25 [Fatigue] ) which allows two characters to switch places while ignoring Zone of Control as long as neither character is stunned, rooted or otherwise disabled.

modifiers.name: Shield Expert
modifiers.description: The shield defense bonus is increased by 25% . This also applies to the additional defense bonus of the Shieldwall skill. Shield damage received is reduced by 50% to a minimum of 1. The 'Knock Back' skill gains +15% chance to hit.

modifiers.name: Spear Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Spearwall is no longer disabled once an opponent manages to overcome it. Instead, Spearwall continues to give free attacks on any further opponent attempting to enter the zone of control. Spearwall can be activated when being already adjacent to opponents. The Spetum and Warfork no longer have a penalty for attacking targets directly adjacent.

modifiers.name: Steel Brow
modifiers.description: Hits to the head no longer cause critical damage to this character, which also lowers the risk of sustaining debilitating head injuries . Note: Steel Brow negates the additional damage provided by Brute or Chop .

modifiers.name: Student
modifiers.description: Gain additional 20% [Experience] from battle. At the eleventh character level, you gain an additional perk point and this perk becomes inert. Playing the Manhunters origin, your [Background 60] Indebted get the perk point refunded at the seventh character level.

modifiers.name: Sword Mastery
modifiers.description: Skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Riposte no longer has a penalty to hitchance. Split and Swing gain a +10% chance to hit. Gash has 50% lower threshold to inflict injuries (+17%).

modifiers.name: Taunt
modifiers.description: Unlocks the Taunt skill (costs 4 [Action Points] and 15 [Fatigue] , maximum range 3) which makes the targeted opponent take offensive actions instead of defensive ones, and attack the taunting character over another, potentially more vulnerable one. Note: this only works if the taunted enemy is able to engage the taunting character, ranged or melee. And will be ignored if there is no unobstructed path. While active, the character attracts more attention from enemies and higher Resolve further increases the attraction.

modifiers.name: Throwing Mastery
modifiers.description: Throwing weapons skills build up 25% less Fatigue. Damage is increased by 30% when attacking at 2 tiles of distance. Damage is increased by 20% when attacking at 3 tiles of distance. Note: Throwable consumables and Slings cost less Fatigue to use. Slings receive damage bonus as well.

modifiers.name: Underdog
modifiers.description: The defense malus due to being surrounded by opponents no longer applies to this character. If an attacker has the Backstabber perk, the effect of that perk is negated, and the normal defense malus due to being surrounded is applied instead.

modifiers.name: Athletic
modifiers.description: Builds up 2 less [Fatigue] for each tile travelled

modifiers.name: Deathwish
modifiers.description: No morale check is triggered upon losing [Hitpoints] hitpoints.

modifiers.name: Determined
modifiers.description: Starts combat at confident morale if his mood permits it.

modifiers.name: Disloyal
modifiers.description: 100% more likely to desert if unhappy.

modifiers.name: Gluttonous
modifiers.description: Eats 3, instead of 2 food per day.

modifiers.name: Impatient
modifiers.description: Always acts first in the first round of combat ( +1000 [Initiative] ).

modifiers.name: Insecure
modifiers.description: Will never be of confident morale.

modifiers.name: Iron Jaw
modifiers.description: The threshold to sustain injuries on getting hit is increased by 25% .

modifiers.name: Irrational
modifiers.description: Has +10 or -10 [Resolve] randomly at every morale check.

modifiers.name: Loyal
modifiers.description: 50% less likely to desert if unhappy.

modifiers.name: Lucky
modifiers.description: 10% chance to require the attacker to make 2 successful attack rolls to hit you

modifiers.name: Spartan
modifiers.description: Eats 1, instead of 2 food per day.

modifiers.name: Superstitious
modifiers.description: -10 [Resolve] at morale checks against fear, panic or mind-control effects.

modifiers.name: Survivor
modifiers.description: 90% chance to survive if reduced to 0 [Hitpoints]

modifiers.name: Team Player
modifiers.description: Has a 50% lower chance to inflict friendly fire.
